title: Regional Inequality from Outer Space
source: Lessmann and Seidel (2017), European Economic Review 92, 110-132; replicated in Python.

light_elasticity.note: Light-to-GDP elasticity (beta_1) across selected columns of Table 1. RE col 7 = preferred prediction model; FE col 2 = clean within-region elasticity used for the Conley robustness check.

kuznets_cubic.note: Cubic in log GDP per capita with country and period fixed effects (Table 3). Sign pattern + / - / + is an N-shape.

determinants.note: Coefficient on each determinant added to the Kuznets cubic with country + period FE (Table 4). Positive = associated with more regional inequality.

conley.note: Spatial-HAC (Conley) standard errors for the clean light elasticity beta = 0.190 (FE column 2), at three great-circle cutoff radii, versus the naive iid standard error.

regional_vs_personal.note: OLS slope of household-income (personal) Gini on the regional Gini, across 144 countries (Figure 5a).

regions_default.note: Six illustrative regions for the inequality-index builder. Incomes in thousands of USD per capita; populations in millions. Spread chosen so the weighted Gini lands near a realistic mid-range value.
